title: Misatos Kiss
wikitext:
[]: Misato's Kiss             Quote Post by SleepS » Fri Feb 19, 2010 2:16 pm I've been lurking around these boards for about a year, and posting a little as of recently. I haven't seen a thread about this and searched, so I thought I'd ask for my own interest and love of Misato. Does anyone want to point me to a thread or explain the significance of Misato kissing Shinji in EoE? Artistically and character-wise? It always is very powerful and seems very important whenever I watch the movie. What are your thoughs?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Feb 19, 2010 4:22 pm It was surprising to see -- but understandable in the circumstances : surely a chance to dip his wick with a hot mama would motivate any 14 year old boy. Its potency is lost, however, if you read episode 23 as the Big Irony Bomb already being set off.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Feb 19, 2010 4:22 pm Shinji was a useless heap of apathy, Misato needed to give him a jumpstart and some incentive (her dialogue).

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Feb 19, 2010 4:34 pm Like most important events in Evangelion, the kiss has meaning on multiple levels and in multiple contexts. On a lower level, we have Misato appealing to Shinji's baser instincts; offerring "service" in return for services rendered—"we'll do the rest when you get back". However on another level, we have Misato finally acting on her long dormant desires. On yet another, Misato and Shinji's personal relationship is once again coming second to their professional one here; that is until Misato's kiss substantially confuses the boundary between the two. There's truly a lot going on in this scene. I don't have any good thread references at the moment, but perhaps someone can recommend some. If not , we could always have a fruitful discussion here. But I think, before we do in earnest, someone is going to have to post "that pic".

[]:             Quote Post by planet news » Fri Feb 19, 2010 5:23 pm Wow . I think that the intent is pretty clear, at least by Anno, because these kinds of things don't just get mistakenly drawn. I doubt that the chair was placed because they didn't want to disturb the previous continuity of the room or balance the composition or something else like that. On first watch however, I didn't think "sexual". I though "hug".

[]:             Quote Post by SleepS » Fri Feb 19, 2010 6:02 pm Thanks for the threads OMF! I've only started reading them, but they're very interesting (Yay for another couple hours devoted to going through these novels. Lol!) Personally, I thought it was simply Misato's usual (only?) way of expression through sex or sexy like things. But my conclusions are usually much more simple-minded and off base then what other posters come up with (hence the thread).

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Feb 20, 2010 10:34 am Hate to be a bit off-topic, but... One day, her kiss will be mine... As for Episode 23, good catch...

[]:             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Sun Feb 21, 2010 11:21 am I see the kiss being a way to show Shinji that she isn't such a "cruel" person after all the things she put him through. Shinji questioning her in ep.24 how she could be so cruel. She is not under any orders to force Shinji into doing anything, she just wants him to do what he feels is right. In stead of giving cold words of athority, she gives him a taste of one thing that he desired. Not to mention, promising the good ol' "in-out in-out" after he is done after doing what he must do.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Feb 21, 2010 11:55 am konfunoodle wrote: I see the kiss being a way to show Shinji that she isn't such a "cruel" person after all the things she put him through.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Feb 21, 2010 12:12 pm konfunoodle wrote: a taste of one thing that he desired.

[]:             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Sun Feb 21, 2010 1:39 pm Reichu wrote: konfunoodle wrote: I see the kiss being a way to show Shinji that she isn't such a "cruel" person after all the things she put him through.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Feb 21, 2010 1:47 pm konfunoodle wrote: ya, I meant she was showing a kinder side of her than always yelling at Shinji to do a mission

[]:             Quote Post by Killericon » Sun Feb 21, 2010 2:54 pm I think that it was her finally accepting herself for what she is. The entire series there's the obvious tension between proper Misato and slutty Misato, and while I want to make it clear that I respect Misato a hell of a lot more than this will make it sound like I do, but she was finally accepting that her slutty/sensual side of her is the true side of her.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Feb 21, 2010 3:00 pm I'm not sure this is correct. Misato has been living life on the wild side for quite some time; actively avoiding being proper. Rather, I think it is her feelings for Shinji that she finally comes to accept in her final moments.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sun Feb 21, 2010 4:30 pm What I was thinking about during this scene was, would she actually have sex with Shinji? I mean, it could of just been a moral boost and she was totally just bluffing. But it's just weird to think about. Then again Shinji would probably run away if he got the chance.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sun Feb 21, 2010 6:19 pm Hatsumi92 wrote: What I was thinking about during this scene was, would she actually have sex with Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sun Feb 21, 2010 8:23 pm Well she does have standards, in fact in episode 2 didn't she say she'd never hit on a boy as young as Shinji? But then again that didn't stop her teasing him in EoE. Oh and by "Weird" i kind of ment weird that she'd have sex with a 14 year old boy, but yeah i doubt it'd be weird to her. But i guess saving the world would be a good enough reason to get some.

[]:             Quote Post by SleepS » Sun Feb 21, 2010 8:26 pm Maybe she didn't know any other way to say goodbye, seeing as of how accustomed she is to this level of intimacy in life?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Feb 22, 2010 2:05 am Hatsumi92 wrote: Well she does have standards, in fact in episode 2 didn't she say she'd never hit on a boy as young as Shinji?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Feb 22, 2010 3:51 am She's thinking of him as cute right from their first meeting.
